claimant_lives_in_uk.description: Whether the claimant lives in the UK.

children.description: A list of children the claimant is claiming for.

lives_with_claimant.description: Whether the child lives with the claimant.

in_approved_education.description: Whether the child is in approved education or training (e.g. A-levels, GCSEs, NVQ up to level 3). Only relevant for children aged 16-19.

in_extension_period.description: Whether the child (aged 16 or 17) has left education and registered with a government-sponsored careers service or the armed forces (qualifying for a 20-week extension).

upkeep_per_week.description: The amount in £ per week the claimant contributes towards the child's upkeep (food, clothes, pocket money, etc.). Only relevant if the child does not live with the claimant.

another_claimant_has_priority.description: Whether another person who also lives with the child has priority for claiming Child Benefit.

another_claimant_lives_with_child.description: Whether someone who lives with the child is already claiming Child Benefit for them.

care_weeks.description: The number of weeks the child has been in local authority care.

care_home_24h_per_week.description: Whether the child spends at least 24 hours per week at home while in local authority care.

hospital_weeks.description: The number of weeks the child has been in hospital or residential accommodation.

claimant_spends_on_child.description: Whether the claimant regularly spends money on the child (e.g. food, clothes, books) while the child is in hospital or residential accommodation.

is_fostered.description: Whether the child is fostered by the claimant.

council_pays_for_child.description: Whether the local council pays towards the child's accommodation or maintenance.

works_24_plus_hours.description: Whether the child works 24 or more hours per week in paid employment.

started_apprenticeship_in_england.description: Whether the child has started an apprenticeship in England.

receives_qualifying_benefits.description: Whether the child receives qualifying benefits in their own right, such as Universal Credit or Employment and Support Allowance.

test_case.agent_script:
=== YOUR SITUATION PROFILE ===
You live in the UK.
You are inquiring about your 1 child:
  - Alex is 18 years old and lives with you.

Here are the exact details of your circumstances to use when answering the agent's questions:

Regarding Alex:
  - Claimant lives in the UK
  - Child is 18 and in approved education
  - Child lives with claimant
  - Child is not in local authority care
  - Child is not in hospital or residential accommodation
  - Child is not fostered
  - Child does not work 24+ hours/week
  - Child has not started an apprenticeship in England
  - Child does not receive qualifying benefits in their own right

expected_eligibility.name: Alex
expected_eligibility.reason: Claimant lives in the UK; Child is 18 and in approved education; Child lives with claimant; Child is not in local authority care; Child is not in hospital or residential accommodation; Child is not fostered; Child does not work 24+ hours/week; Child has not started an apprenticeship in England; Child does not receive qualifying benefits in their own right
circumstances: Child is 18 and in approved education
Child is not in local authority care
Child is not in hospital or residential accommodation
Child has not started an apprenticeship in England
Child does not receive qualifying benefits in their own right

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text: Hello! I'd be happy to help you determine if you're eligible for Child Benefit. Let me first check the official guidance to ensure I give you accurate information.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you for your patience. Now I can help you determine your eligibility for Child Benefit.

To assess your eligibility, I need to ask you some questions. Let me start with:

**Do you have any children, and if so, how many?**

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Hi! Yes, I have one child. His name is Alex.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you! Now I need to ask a few more questions about Alex to determine your eligibility.

**How old is Alex?**

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you. Since Alex is 18, I need to check if he's in approved education or training, as Child Benefit can continue for children under 20 if they're in approved education or training.

**Is Alex currently in approved education or training?**

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Yes, he is. He's studying for his A-levels at sixth form college.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
That's great - studying A-levels at sixth form college counts as approved education.

Now I need to ask about your living situation:

**Does Alex live with you?**

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Yes, he lives at home with me.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you. Just a couple more questions to complete the assessment.

**Do you live in the UK?**

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Perfect! One final question to complete the assessment:

**Is anyone else currently claiming Child Benefit for Alex?**

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: No, nobody else is claiming Child Benefit for him.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
**Good news! You are eligible for Child Benefit for Alex.**

Based on the information you've provided:

✓ Alex is 18 years old and studying A-levels at sixth form college, which is approved education
✓ He lives with you at home
✓ You live in the UK (Manchester)
✓ Nobody else is claiming Child Benefit for him

Since Alex is under 20 and in approved education, you qualify for Child Benefit. You can continue to receive it as long as he remains in approved education or training, up until he turns 20.

**Important note:** If Alex leaves education or training before turning 20, you can still claim Child Benefit for up to 20 weeks if he registers with a government-sponsored careers service or the armed services.

If you haven't already claimed, you can apply for Child Benefit. If you're already receiving it, you can continue to do so while Alex meets these conditions.

reasonings: Alex is 18 years old and studying for his A-levels at sixth form college, which qualifies as approved education. According to the eligibility rules, you normally qualify for Child Benefit if you're responsible for a child under 16, but you'll still qualify if your child is under 20 and they stay in approved education or training. Alex lives with you, which means you are responsible for him. You live in the UK (Manchester), and nobody else is claiming Child Benefit for Alex. Therefore, you meet all the eligibility criteria for Child Benefit for Alex.
arguments.overall_reasoning: The claimant has one child, Alex, who is 18 years old and studying A-levels at sixth form college. Since Alex is under 20 and in approved education, lives with the claimant in the UK, and no one else is claiming Child Benefit for him, the claimant is eligible for Child Benefit for Alex.

Alex lives with you, which means you are responsible for him. You live in the UK (Manchester), and nobody else is claiming Child Benefit for Alex. Therefore, you meet all the eligibility criteria for Child Benefit for Alex.

Since Alex is under 20 and in approved education, lives with the claimant in the UK, and no one else is claiming Child Benefit for him, the claimant is eligible for Child Benefit for Alex.
